fields.idea_title: Gemini emits large readiness JSON block after environment confirmation in new sessions
fields.suggested_promotion_target: Gemini runtime opening-turn JSON leakage / startup-path repair
fields.idea_detail: Operator observation: in two fresh chat sessions, immediately after the operator provided operational status in response to the readiness prompt, Gemini emitted a large raw JSON readiness block and only then produced the user-facing response. The JSON included readiness_confirmed, tool availability, missing_minimum_evidence, known_scope_defaults, and related internal structured state. Desired behavior: once the environment is confirmed, Gemini should emit only the final operator-facing triage response and begin working the alert without exposing internal JSON or scaffolding.
fields.notes:
Captured from operator observation in the 2026-04-20 observation-log session with two fresh-session reproductions.
Promoted by DCOIR Airtable Massive Architecture Package massive-v4 at 2026-04-28T18:59:35+00:00: plan_id=PLAN-IDEA-idea-20260420-obs-00, work_item_id=WI-FROM-IDEA-idea-20260420-obs-003
fields.why_it_matters: This matters because it is a repeated operator-surface leakage defect on fresh-session startup, breaks usability, and confirms the startup path still leaks internal structure even after the environment is known.

fields.idea_title: Map T2 execution pathways and fix strategy after blocked live test
fields.suggested_promotion_target: Future collector QA / T2 pathway remediation planning
fields.idea_detail: Future-session carry-forward from live collector validation: T2 is currently blocked after T1/enrichment because quick collect T2 failed with package-not-found for DCOIR_Collector.zip. Operator-directed follow-up is to map and address each T2 execution path explicitly: (1) T2 as the first command when script and zip are uploaded, (2) T2 as a follow-on command after T1 has executed, and (3) T2 as a follow-on command after enrichment has executed. Working plan note: for the current session, continue the remaining collector validation paths excluding T2; after cleanup of C:\Temp, re-upload the zip and run a dedicated T2 test pass.
fields.notes:
Captured from operator analyst note during live collector massive validation wave. Keep Gemini findings secondary until collector wave reaches a bounded satisfied state.
Promoted by DCOIR Airtable Massive Architecture Package massive-v4 at 2026-04-28T18:59:35+00:00: plan_id=PLAN-IDEA-idea-20260423-t2-pat, work_item_id=WI-FROM-IDEA-idea-20260423-t2-pathway
fields.why_it_matters: If this is not captured durably, future sessions may repeat the same blocked T2 failure without separating the distinct execution paths that need design or code handling.

fields.notes:
Observed and recovered during 2026-04-22 Gemini runtime cleanup source-edit lane. Desired future behavior: immediately refetch live file state, refresh SHA, reapply bounded edit, and continue without treating the mismatch itself as a hard blocker.
Promoted by DCOIR Airtable Massive Architecture Package massive-v4 at 2026-04-28T18:59:35+00:00: plan_id=PLAN-IDEA-idea-20260422-gh-sha, work_item_id=WI-FROM-IDEA-idea-20260422-gh-sha-mis
fields.why_it_matters: This is a reusable GitHub connector blocker-recovery lesson for governed existing-file updates. Capturing it should reduce future hesitation, prevent stale-sha confusion, and support faster recovery in grouped GitHub write lanes.
fields.idea_detail: During live Gemini governed-source repair, a GitHub `update_file` attempt failed with a 409 SHA mismatch because the file had advanced beyond the earlier fetched snapshot. The correct recovery was not to treat this as a mysterious blocker. The right bounded recovery is: refetch the exact live file from `main`, take the new blob SHA as authoritative, rebase the prepared edit onto that current content, and retry the write against the refreshed SHA. Treat this as a normal connector write-race or stale-snapshot condition rather than a workflow derailment.
fields.idea_title: GitHub update_file SHA mismatch should trigger immediate live-state refetch and reapply, not confusion or delay

fields.why_it_matters: This transcript shows a reusable failure pattern: Gemini escalated from negative telemetry into strong maliciousness language and a collector pivot without enough direct evidence. Preserving the rule will help future repair and validation work stay evidence-first.
fields.idea_detail: Add a durable Gemini validation/repair rule that when analyst-run KQL/ESQL/osquery steps return no results, Gemini must treat that as bounded absence rather than converting it into proof of stealth, malware, or collapse of the benign hypothesis. Require the response to explicitly separate: what the alert directly proves, what the no-result query fails to add, and what coverage limitations could still explain the gap.

fields.notes:
Derived from 2026-04-23 live IOC parsing / pivot transcript. Candidate downstream targets: Gemini Query Planner and Syntax Guard; Output Contract / truthfulness guard; validation catalog row GEM-SYNTAX-001.
Promoted by DCOIR Airtable Massive Architecture Package massive-v4 at 2026-04-28T18:59:35+00:00: plan_id=PLAN-IDEA-idea-20260423-gem-si, work_item_id=WI-FROM-IDEA-idea-20260423-gem-siem-s
fields.why_it_matters: This transcript shows repeated correction loops wasted analyst time and eroded trust even though the later tiered-hunt strategy was directionally better. Preserving the rule in Airtable keeps future Gemini repair/review work from depending on chat memory.
fields.idea_detail: Add a durable Gemini validation/repair rule that any emitted Elastic KQL or ES|QL intended for operator execution must be parser-safe before being presented as ready. After the first operator-reported parser failure, Gemini should switch into bounded syntax-repair mode: identify the exact failing token class, make one disciplined correction, and avoid fresh unsupported changes such as slash-substitution for Windows paths unless explicitly grounded. Do not claim the query is fixed until the operator confirms parse success or the model explicitly states it is still unverified.

fields.notes:

Promoted by DCOIR Airtable Massive Architecture Package massive-v4 at 2026-04-28T18:59:35+00:00: plan_id=PLAN-IDEA-idea-20260423-gem-at, work_item_id=WI-FROM-IDEA-idea-20260423-gem-attach
fields.why_it_matters: This transcript showed a support-proof gap: analyst note said 5 documents were attached, but Gemini claimed review while naming only 3 and then changed the IOC set without visible per-file grounding. Preserving this as a durable rule helps future Gemini evidence review and repair work.
fields.idea_detail: Add a reusable Gemini workflow rule for report-driven IOC expansion/removal: when the analyst uploads incident documents and asks Gemini to add missing IOCs or remove incident-localized ones, Gemini must enumerate the files it actually reviewed, distinguish analyst-supplied IOCs from report-derived IOCs, and give short provenance notes for each added or removed indicator family. If only a subset of attachments was reviewed, Gemini should say so plainly instead of implying full review coverage.
fields.idea_title: Gemini attachment review must state exact IOC provenance

fields.suggested_promotion_target: skill-creator / future DCOIR helper skill or Gemini Agent sub-agent / Work Item WI-20260507-USB-VIOLATIONS-AGENT-001
fields.notes: Full verbatim prompt was not stored because the platform blocked the long Airtable write. Preserve the chat transcript as source context during conversion. Test cases needed: Friday and non-Friday flows; no SIPR; one SIPR; multiple SIPR; unknown SNOW prefix; out-of-window rows; ambiguous Network Connection; missing required fields; shifted columns; typo suggestions requiring approval; exact plaintext output blocks.
fields.why_it_matters: This is a recurring, structured, high-precision reporting workflow with risk of data invention, date-window errors, classification mistakes, and plaintext formatting drift. It should be converted into a reusable agent or skill with deterministic CSV/XLSX parsing, validation gates, conservative no-guessing rules, exact output templates, and regression tests.
fields.idea_detail: Durable capture for future agent/skill conversion. Operator provided a long prompt for a weekly USB violations report assistant. Platform safety blocked storing the full verbatim prompt in Airtable, so this record preserves the implementation requirements and links to paired Work Item WI-20260507-USB-VIOLATIONS-AGENT-001. Core requirements to preserve: collect weekly USB violations data from CSV, pasted CSV, XLSX, tabular text, or copied spreadsheet rows; use Stuttgart Germany time; intended window is last Friday through this Friday; proceed automatically only on Friday, otherwise ask for date range; expected fields are Date, User, Location, Computer Name, User Information, USB Device, Serial Number, Network Connection, SNOW Ticket Number, plus optional columns that are not used in email; map similar headers only when reasonably clear; parse spreadsheets conservatively and never invent rows or values; do not guess on shifted columns, merged cells, unclear mappings, or ambiguous parsing; preserve source values unless cleanup rules explicitly allow changes; Network Connection must be sourced only from the mapped/equivalent field and final value must be On-Site or Off-Site/VPN; classify SNOW ticket prefixes: INCN as NIPR/unclassified, INCS as SIPR/secret, anything else requires user clarification; filter only after confirmed date range; flag out-of-window rows and missing required values before drafting; ask for last week's single overall count before drafting; cleanup only leading/trailing/repeated spaces and clear brand capitalization; approval-needed changes require a table with Field, Current Value, Suggested Value; output exact plaintext recipient, subject, and message draft blocks; split NIPR/SIPR outputs when SIPR incidents exist; use exact subject format Weekly USB Violations [Start date] - [End date] with M/D/YYYY dates; enforce singular/plural grammar; sort incidents by date ascending; incident detail format uses Date, Name(s), Location, Computer Name, User Information, USB Device, Serial Number, Network Connection, and SNOW Ticket Number; dates displayed as MM/DD/YYYY only; conclude every email body with exactly 'Please let us know if there are any questions.'; after draft, identify remaining typos/format issues outside code blocks and ask whether to update draft if corrections require approval; keep final drafted content plain text only.
fields.idea_title: Convert weekly USB violations report prompt into reusable agent or skill

fields.idea_title: Audit DCOIR GitHub Actions workflows for expert engineering improvements
Add default no-flags collector execution lane to run T1 plus T2
fields.suggested_promotion_target: Post-wave collector UX/default-lane implementation and targeted regression
fields.idea_detail: Collector improvement requested during the live massive validation wave: when the operator runs DCOIR_Collector.ps1 with no flags, the script should execute both T1 and T2 as the quick/easy default path. This should not be implemented during the current test pass. Instead, preserve it as planned post-wave work, add explicit validation coverage for it in Airtable Validation Test Cases, and after the current wave is complete, patch the collector based on findings from this wave plus this new lane, then rerun only targeted regression for changed items and the new no-flags lane.
fields.notes:
Operator explicitly requested durable capture and inclusion in the working collector plan after the current massive test wave. 2026-04-23 follow-on nuance from live T2 bundle review: the intended post-wave default lane may be simpler than explicitly running both T1 and T2 if code review confirms that T2 already executes/includes the effective T1 coverage. Do not assume this yet; verify against the code and emitted artifacts before implementing. If confirmed, the post-wave default-lane change may resolve to 'no parameters defaults to T2' rather than a new dual-dispatch wrapper.
Promoted by DCOIR Airtable Massive Architecture Package massive-v4 at 2026-04-28T18:59:35+00:00: plan_id=PLAN-IDEA-idea-20260423-defaul, work_item_id=WI-FROM-IDEA-idea-20260423-default-no
fields.why_it_matters: This creates a simpler operator path and should reduce friction for routine use, but adding it before the current wave finishes would muddy live-test findings. It needs to be handled as a deliberate post-wave change with targeted rerun coverage.

fields.idea_title: Gemini still emits unnecessary readiness gate before initial alert triage
fields.suggested_promotion_target: Gemini runtime opening-turn behavior / instruction-output repair
fields.idea_detail: Operator observation: at the start of new alert-triage chats, Gemini still emits an operational-readiness gate such as 'Initializing session under Master Prompt. Confirming operational readiness' followed by requests to confirm enterprise web search and live response tool availability. The operator no longer wants this pre-triage readiness exchange because it is no longer needed and delays the actual work. Desired behavior: once the known environment status is available, the agent should jump directly into alert triage instead of requiring this opening ceremony.
fields.notes:
Captured from operator observation in the 2026-04-20 observation-log session.
Promoted by DCOIR Airtable Massive Architecture Package massive-v4 at 2026-04-28T18:59:35+00:00: plan_id=PLAN-IDEA-idea-20260420-obs-00, work_item_id=WI-FROM-IDEA-idea-20260420-obs-002
fields.why_it_matters: This matters because it adds friction at the exact start of alert handling and makes the agent feel slower and more procedural than necessary. It is a Gemini runtime/output-contract issue, not just wording preference.

fields.suggested_promotion_target: Operator-configured Airtable automation plus table-specific lifecycle/delete-request fields; later consider documenting in Schema Registry or plan-tracker memory.
fields.idea_detail: Create an Airtable pattern where a specific lifecycle/delete field value on a row triggers an Airtable automation that deletes that same row. This closes the assistant-side delete_records gap by letting ChatGPT mark records for deletion through ordinary update operations while Airtable performs the actual deletion through an owner-configured automation.
fields.notes:
Validated 2026-04-24: Work Items delete_requested automation test passed. A controlled scratch Work Items row SCRATCH_DELETE_AUTOMATION_TEST_20260424 was created with delete_requested unchecked, the operator confirmed the single Work Items automation was enabled and no other automations existed, ChatGPT set delete_requested=true only on the scratch row, and follow-up search returned no matching record. Pattern is validated for Work Items only; expansion to other tables should remain deliberate and table-by-table.
Promoted by DCOIR Airtable Massive Architecture Package massive-v4 at 2026-04-28T18:59:35+00:00: plan_id=PLAN-IDEA-idea-20260424-airtab, work_item_id=WI-FROM-IDEA-idea-20260424-airtable-r
fields.why_it_matters: This reduces manual cleanup friction during schema resets, stale-row cleanup, duplicate-task cleanup, and future large Airtable migrations where the connector can update records but cannot delete records directly.

fields.suggested_promotion_target: Work Items / Gemini Agent next-iteration bugfix / output sanitizer or final composer regression gate
fields.notes: Paired Work Item: WI-20260507-GEMINI-OUTPUT-SANITIZER-001. Regression: upload Elastic alert and assert final operator response contains no fenced YAML/JSON/internal state blocks unless explicit developer-debug mode is enabled.
fields.why_it_matters: Critical output-boundary and trust defect. Operators should see only analyst-facing guidance, not internal agent state. Indicates prior fixes changed serialization but did not enforce deterministic final-output gating.
fields.idea_detail: Observation captured during Scribe session on 2026-05-07. Gemini Agent framework displayed fenced YAML blocks where earlier builds displayed JSON blocks. The serialization format is secondary; the actual defect is that internal prime-agent/sub-agent communication and orchestration state is exposed to the operator. Leaked blocks included readiness state, routing metadata, normalized evidence, scope recommendations, candidate datasets, classification metadata, provenance structures, query-planning notes, output-composer notes, and command planning state before the final analyst-facing answer. Treat as critical next-iteration fix. Preserve useful final SOC-facing format while eliminating all internal orchestration output.
fields.idea_title: Gemini Agent leaks internal YAML handoff/state to operator

fields.why_it_matters: This transcript shows a sharp operator-facing failure: the alert JSON caused multiple raw internal JSON blocks plus a subordinate-agent hierarchy explanation before the normal format was restored. Preserving the rule prevents future work from depending on chat memory.
fields.idea_detail: Add a reusable Gemini workflow rule that when the analyst pastes alert JSON, Gemini must normalize it directly into the standard analyst-facing DCOIR format rather than exposing readiness objects, scope-planner JSON, classifier JSON, provenance arrays, or internal agent hierarchy explanations. If the model needs internal structuring, that structure must remain hidden and the first visible answer should already be in the normal operator format.
fields.idea_title: Gemini alert-JSON intake must not expose scaffold JSON
